title: 18-Inch Doll Sofa or Couch
summary: A simple 1x6-and-1x2 doll sofa or matching chair with a sewn box-cushion seat and pillows — a genuine upholstery project alongside the woodworking, sized for 18-inch dolls.
description:
This is a two-part project: a simple wood frame, plus real sewn upholstery rather than a bought cushion pad glued on. Build the wood frame first — arms attached to supports at two different heights (front up 3/4", back up 1/2") to give the finished piece a slight seat-back lean, then the seat back and bottom joined and set inside the arms. **Round the arm tops and the top back piece with a jigsaw** before finishing, since these are the edges most likely to catch on hands or furniture at this small scale.

The sofa and chair share the exact same construction, just at two different sizes — build either one, or both to make a matching two-piece set. **The cushion is where this project becomes a genuine sewing project**: cut fabric pieces for the seat cushion and pillows, sew with right sides together and 1/4" seams, leaving an opening to turn each piece right-side out and stuff.

**Boxing the cushion corners is the one sewing technique worth practicing on scrap fabric first** — pull the corner's seams into alignment, mark a line perpendicular to the seam where the diagonal measures 1", and sew along it; done correctly, this squares off what would otherwise be a flat, floppy cushion into a true box shape that sits properly in the seat opening.

tools.note: Rounding the arm and back corners.

steps.title: Attach supports to the arms
steps.body: Attach the supports to the arms so the front sits up 3/4" and the back sits up 1/2". Use 1-1/4" nails and glue, or pocket holes if available.

steps.title: Build the seat
steps.body: Attach the seat back to the seat bottom with 1-1/4" nails and glue.

steps.title: Assemble and round the corners
steps.body: Place the seat inside the arms and attach through the arms and into the supports with 1-1/4" nails and glue. Round the arm tops and top back piece with a jigsaw to prevent sharp edges.

steps.title: Sew and stuff the cushions
steps.body: Cut fabric pieces for the seat cushion and pillows. Sew right sides together with 1/4" seams, leaving an opening to turn right-side out. Box the seat cushion corners by aligning the seams and sewing a 1" perpendicular line at each corner. Stuff pillows with polyester stuffing and hand-stitch openings closed.

images.alt: Small wood-framed doll sofa with a sewn cushion
